title: Executive Order 11697—Inspection by Department of Agriculture of Income Tax Returns Made Under the Internal Revenue Code of 1954 of Persons Having Farm Operations

flags.justification: A narrow, specific authorization for Agriculture to inspect certain tax data 'for statistical purposes only,' bounded by a cross-referenced Treasury Decision.
Compliance with the Secretary of the Treasury's specific rules and regulations is required, providing a real, cross-referenced compliance framework.
Single, narrow purpose: inter-agency statistical data access.
Bounded by a specific referenced Treasury Decision with its own rules and 'for statistical purposes only' limitation.
A government-to-government data-sharing arrangement, not a third-party compliance regime with an incentive structure.
A standing data-sharing arrangement for an ongoing statistical need, evergreen by nature.
This requires compliance with Treasury's own existing rules and regulations rather than bypassing them.
A government-to-government data-sharing arrangement with no obvious third-party monitor.
A narrow, technical inter-agency data-sharing arrangement; no broad authority claim.
Access is carefully bounded to occur within IRS premises under IRS/Treasury's own rules, not overriding IRS's mandate.
Narrowly scoped to specific data, taxable years, and purpose, bounded by Treasury's existing regulatory framework — not a broad undermining carve-out.
